item.title: Maybe the Market Was Never Expensive
item.description: If only we had known what was coming.

item.title: AI Health Startup Wants to Assist Half of Latin American Doctors
item.description: An Andreessen Horowitz-backed healthcare startup born in Latin America wants to put its AI assistant in the hands of half the region’s 1.9 million doctors by the end of 2027, a bet that technology can help bridge a shortage of medical professionals across strained health systems.

item.title: A First-Ever Default Shakes an $80 Billion Corner of Muni Market
item.description: More than two decades after Wall Street started pumping out a new type of bonds — those backed by the legal-settlement payments governments receive from cigarette companies — one batch has finally been driven into a default. It almost certainly won’t be the last.

item.title: Operar día y noche: el cambio cultural que se aproxima a Wall Street
item.description: Las entidades financieras tradicionales están empezando a evaluar modelos de negociación ininterrumpida de diversos activos, de una forma que lleva años revolucionando los horarios en el sector cripto.

item.title: Capital Factory CEO Killed in Private-Jet Crash in Texas
item.description: Joshua Baer, the founder and chief executive officer of the venture capital firm Capital Factory, died Tuesday in a plane crash in South Texas, the Austin American-Statesman reported.

item.title: Wall Street Revives Risky Loan Deals That Banks Couldn’t Sell
item.description: Wall Street banks are looking to make the most of rising demand for risky debt with a raft of leveraged loan sales that they previously couldn’t sell.

item.title: Brazilian Nickel Seeks Anchor Investor for $1.4 Billion Mine
item.description: Brazilian Nickel Ltd. is seeking an anchor investor to help attract more equity funding for its planned $1.4 billion nickel and cobalt mine in northeastern Brazil.

item.title: India’s Largest Bourse NSE Files Draft Papers for Landmark IPO
item.description: National Stock Exchange of India Ltd., the operator of the world’s busiest derivatives market, has filed draft documents for what’s on track to be one of the largest initial public offerings in India’s history.

item.title: Brevan Howard Prepares to Start Backing External Stocks Traders
item.description: Brevan Howard Asset Management is preparing to give money to stocks-focused hedge funds, adding to a wave of giant firms forking over cash to outside talent.

item.title: Fed Expected to Hold Rates in Warsh's First Decision
item.description: Kevin Warsh Photographer: Graeme Sloan/Bloomberg

item.title: Russian Gasoline Prices Skyrocket as Ukraine Strikes Refineries
item.description: Russian gasoline prices advanced further, with drivers in the summer vacation season facing headaches after some filling stations across the nation limited sales due to Ukrainian attacks on refineries.

item.title: Corn Ships Cross Hormuz Toward Iran, UAE Ahead of Trump Deal
item.description: A fully-loaded corn ship bound for Iran entered the Persian Gulf after crossing the US blockade line outside the Strait of Hormuz, ahead of the US-Iran interim peace deal aimed at reopening the waterway.

item.title: Copper Miner Antofagasta Seeks to Link Ore Sales to Spot Market
item.description: Chile’s Antofagasta Plc has approached Chinese copper smelters with a proposal to price contractual sales of copper ore using spot-market indexes, a fresh sign that a decades-old system based on fixed-price deals is under strain.

item.title: Dark Oil Tanker Transits Keep Oil Flowing Through Hormuz Ahead of Peace Deal
item.description: Tankers crossing the Strait of Hormuz with signals turned off are keeping this month’s oil flows at more robust levels than earlier in the Middle East conflict, as shippers await the signing of a US-Iran peace deal this week.

item.title: Mutares Is Said to Mull Sale or IPO of Portuguese Firm Efacec
item.description: Mutares SE & Co. is exploring options for Portuguese engineering group Efacec, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: BioNTech Investor Förtsch Says Founders’ Spinout Raises Concerns
item.description: German media mogul and investor Bernd Förtsch is calling on BioNTech SE to provide more details on the planned departure of its founders and the transfer of assets to their new venture.

item.title: Oil Industry Makes Tentative Moves Ahead of Deal to Open Hormuz
item.description: The oil and shipping industry remains largely in wait-and-see mode ahead of Friday’s deadline to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, although there are some tentative signs of preparations as a handful of ships begin rerouting toward the Middle East while Iranian tankers laden with oil move out.

item.title: France Adopts Law to Ease EDF Grip on Hydropower Market, Spur Investment on New Generation Capacity
item.description: The French Parliament adopted a bill to loosen Electricite de France SA’s grip on the hydropower market, a move aimed at ending a spat with the European Union and unlocking billions of euros in investment for new generation capacity.

item.title: VSCO Challenges Adobe With New App and a $500 Plan for Pros
item.description: VSCO, the photography platform best known for its popular image filters, is introducing a more advanced iOS app and an all-in-one subscription plan, moves that put it in direct competition with Adobe Inc. and other brands offering professional-grade creative software.

item.title: US Order on Anthropic Models Signals New Era for AI Controls
item.description: The confusing matter of Mythos.

item.title: French Senators Warn of Wealth ‘Black Box’ in Tax System
item.description: French senators investigating how the rich minimize tax bills warned of increasing opacity of their assets and called for more reporting by the wealthiest.

item.title: ASML CEO Warns of Possible Supply Constraints on Musk’s Terafab
item.description: ASML Holding NV Chief Executive Officer Christophe Fouquet said the chip equipment maker has to make sure it doesn’t experience supply constraints when servicing new projects such as Elon Musk’s Terafab.

item.title: Billions in SpaceX Trades Jolt ETFs Including Cathie Wood’s ARKK
item.description: A series of unusual multibillion-dollar flows has rippled through the exchange-traded fund market in the past week, suggesting some major investors used a contentious method to get exposure to the initial public offering of SpaceX. At least one fund manager placed temporary restrictions on its product in a bid to curb the practice.

item.title: Bulgaria to Oppose EU Sanctions Against Russian Patriarch Kirill
item.description: Bulgaria’s new government will oppose sanctions against the head of the Russian Orthodox Church included in the latest European Union package of measures targeting Moscow, the country’s foreign minister said.

item.title: Citadel Securities Saw ‘Astronomical’ Retail Trading in SpaceX
item.description: Retail investors poured into US stocks at a record pace on the day of SpaceX’s blockbuster initial public offering and are increasingly driving the broader rally in equities, according to Citadel Securities.

item.title: BofA Public Finance Head Sees AI Boosting Underwriting Dominance
item.description: Matthew McQueen, who oversees Bank of America Corp.’s public finance department, is looking to artificial intelligence to expand the bank’s underwriting business across the country.

item.title: BYD Takes On Luxury EV Rivals in China With More Affordable SUV
item.description: BYD Co. showed off its largest electric car to date under its namesake brand, as the Chinese automaker attempts to challenge premium rivals with a more affordable sport utility vehicle.

item.title: Iraq Takes Steps to Boost Oil Exports When Hormuz Reopens
item.description: Iraq is preparing to boost oil exports from its southern ports with a formal deal to open the Strait of Hormuz scheduled to be signed on Friday.

item.title: US Oil Stockpiles Fall to Critical Lows as War Spurs Exports
item.description: Inventories at the US’s largest commercial crude oil storage hub fell to the lowest since 2014, as surging exports during the Iran war drain supplies in America.

item.title: US Shale Stays in the Money Despite Oil Price Drop
item.description: Long-dated prices still support drilling as buyers seek alternatives to Middle East barrels.
